summary: Validated P0 fixes for StationCab driver dashboard: (1) Active ride action buttons (Je suis arrivé/Démarrer/Terminer + Waze + Google Maps) repositioned at top of card and visible without scroll, (2) Passenger cancellation no longer crashes the driver dashboard - cancellation toast 'Course annulée par le client' shown, active ride card cleared, driver dashboard remains functional. All 10 backend tests pass (100%). End-to-end UI flow verified.

minor.issue: Returns 500 when ride is already auto-assigned to the calling driver (auto-dispatch is fast). Not user-facing because UI only shows Accepter on pending unassigned rides, but worth turning into a 400 with clear message.
cancelled_by is persisted in DB but stripped by RideResponse model (not in pydantic schema). UI doesn't currently need it, but consider exposing it for analytics.

issues: 'Géolocalisation requise' warning persists in test browser because permission denied - expected in headless tests, not a real bug

passed_tests: Backend: POST /api/rides/{id}/cancel by passenger on pending ride -> 200 status=cancelled
Backend: POST /api/rides/{id}/cancel on accepted ride frees driver (active ride disappears for driver)
Frontend: /auth page renders, driver login redirects to /driver dashboard
Frontend: Driver Dashboard shows stats cards (14.14€ Aujourd'hui, 1 Courses, 1675.67€ Total, 4.4 Note)
Frontend: Active ride card shows action buttons (data-testid=driver-arrived-btn, open-waze-btn, open-gmaps-btn) ABOVE passenger info section
Frontend: arrivedY=673 < passengerY=918 AND arrivedY<844 (viewport height) -> buttons visible without scroll
Frontend: Passenger API-cancel triggers driver-side toast 'Course annulée par le client' within ~5s polling window
Frontend: After cancellation, activeRide card cleared, driver dashboard still functional (availability-switch present), no crash/disconnect
Integration: Firebase-style notification {type: 'ride_cancelled'} received via push channel in console as expected
action_items: OPTIONAL: Add 'cancelled_by' field to RideResponse pydantic model so frontend/analytics can distinguish passenger vs driver cancellations

OPTIONAL: Change POST /api/rides/{id}/accept response from 500 to 400 'ride already assigned' when ride is already in accepted state (currently throws Internal Server Error if a driver tries to accept a ride already assigned to them via auto-dispatch)
critical_code_review_comments: DriverDashboard.js: prevActiveRideRef logic (lines 574-612) correctly detects passenger cancellation by comparing previous status (accepted/arrived/in_progress) against null new value, shows toast + plays sound + sets isAvailable(true). Good defensive UX.
DriverDashboard.js (1124-1208): Action buttons + Waze/Gmaps placed BEFORE address/passenger details inside the active ride Card - confirmed visible without scroll on 390x844 viewport.

server.py cancel_ride endpoint (2528-2583): Correctly sets is_available=True for the driver and dispatches firebase notification to driver on passenger cancel. Notification error wrapped in try/except so it never breaks the cancel transaction. Good.
server.py is still 5236 lines - exceeds 700-line guideline; should be split into routers (rides, auth, drivers, admin).
Backend POST /accept returns 500 on duplicate accept (auto-dispatch race) - swallow with explicit 400 + log message.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (driver dashboard load + active ride layout + cancellation toast all verified)

seed_data_creation: None - test creates throwaway rides via API and cancels them in cleanup
context_for_next_testing_agent: P0 fixes confirmed working. Backend BASE_URL must use https://taxi-connect-47.preview.emergentagent.com and requests need User-Agent header to bypass Cloudflare (error 1010 with default urllib UA). The /api/rides/{id}/accept endpoint returns 500 if a driver is already auto-assigned to the ride - this is a minor issue, not a P0 regression. The 'cancelled_by' field is persisted but not exposed by RideResponse. fetchActiveRide polls roughly every 5-7s, so passenger cancellation detection takes up to ~8s in tests.
rca of the issue: Both P0 fixes are correctly implemented. (1) UI reorganization: Action buttons block moved to top inside CardContent before passenger/address sections. Bounding-box test confirms arrived_btn.top=673 < passengerLabel.top=918 and within 844px viewport. (2) Cancellation detection: prevActiveRideRef stores previous fetch result; when next fetch returns null AND prev had a non-completed active ride, a toast is shown, sound played, and driver marked available. Verified end-to-end via passenger API cancel -> driver UI toast 'Course annulée par le client' within polling window.
